gloss.en: the act of putting a decision or plan into effect

words.example: The implementation of the new system took months.

gloss.en: to put someone in prison

words.example: They were imprisoned for selling drugs.

gloss.en: the state of being put in prison

words.example: He was sentenced to life imprisonment for murder.

gloss.en: the fact of being unable to do something

words.example: Her inability to swim worried the teacher.

gloss.en: not enough or not good enough

words.example: The food supplies were inadequate for everyone.

gloss.en: not suitable for the situation

words.example: His joke was inappropriate at the funeral.

gloss.en: how often something happens

words.example: This area has a high incidence of crime.

gloss.en: wanting or tending to do something

words.example: She was inclined to trust him.

gloss.en: the fact of including someone or something

words.example: His inclusion in the team is in doubt.

gloss.en: to bring something unpleasant on yourself

words.example: You may incur extra costs if you pay late.

gloss.en: a sign that shows how a situation is changing

words.example: The economic indicators are better than expected.

gloss.en: a sign that something is very bad or wrong

words.example: City poverty is a damning indictment of our society.

gloss.en: native to a particular place

words.example: We studied the indigenous people of the region.

gloss.en: to persuade someone to do something

words.example: Nothing would induce me to take the job.

gloss.en: to let yourself enjoy something pleasant

words.example: They went to town to indulge in some shopping.

gloss.en: unfair difference between groups in society

words.example: We must tackle inequality wherever we find it.

gloss.en: well known for being bad or evil
gloss.zh: 臭名昭著的；声名狼藉的

words.example: The general was infamous for his cruelty.

gloss.en: a baby or very young child

words.example: This nursery cares for infants under two.

gloss.en: to spread a disease to a person or thing

words.example: The virus can infect anyone who touches it.

gloss.en: to make someone suffer something unpleasant

words.example: They inflicted a heavy defeat on the home team.

gloss.en: having a lot of influence

words.example: She wrote a highly influential book.

gloss.en: existing as a basic, permanent part of something

words.example: There are risks inherent in this kind of work.

gloss.en: to slow down or prevent something

words.example: Fear can inhibit a child from speaking freely.

gloss.en: to make something begin

words.example: They decided to initiate legal action against him.

gloss.en: to put a drug into the body with a needle

words.example: The nurse will inject the medicine into your arm.

gloss.en: an act of putting a drug into the body

words.example: The doctor gave him an injection for the pain.

gloss.en: an unfair situation or unfair treatment

words.example: We are committed to fighting poverty and injustice.

gloss.en: a person living in a prison or similar place

words.example: The jail has five hundred inmates.

gloss.en: the act of putting one thing inside another

words.example: A check is done before the insertion of the tube.

gloss.en: a person who knows a group from within

words.example: One insider called the situation absolute chaos.

gloss.en: to look closely at something to check it

words.example: The teacher walked around to inspect their work.

gloss.en: an official check to ensure standards are met

words.example: Regular inspections are carried out at the prison.

gloss.en: something that gives you exciting new ideas

words.example: Dreams can be a rich source of inspiration.

gloss.en: a natural way of behaving you are born with

words.example: Her first instinct was to run away.

gloss.en: connected with a large important organization

words.example: The bank attracted many institutional investors.

gloss.en: to formally tell someone to do something

words.example: The letter instructed him to report at once.

gloss.en: important in making something happen
gloss.zh: 起重要作用的；有助益的

words.example: She was instrumental in saving the old library.

gloss.en: not enough for a particular purpose

words.example: There was insufficient time to finish the test.

gloss.en: a rude remark or action meant to offend

words.example: The crowd shouted insults at the police.

gloss.en: complete and not damaged

words.example: Most of the old house remains intact today.

gloss.en: the amount of food or drink you take in

words.example: Doctors advised him to cut his salt intake.

gloss.en: being an essential part of something
gloss.zh: 不可或缺的；构成整体的

words.example: Music is an integral part of the school.

gloss.en: with parts closely connected and working together

words.example: The hospital offers an integrated program of care.

gloss.en: the act of combining things to work together

words.example: The aim is to promote closer economic integration.

gloss.en: the quality of being honest and moral

words.example: People admired her honesty and integrity.

gloss.en: connected with thinking and understanding

words.example: Gifted children often show great intellectual curiosity.

gloss.en: to increase in strength or degree

words.example: The violence intensified during the night.

gloss.en: the quality of being very strong or intense

words.example: We measured the intensity of the light.

gloss.en: involving much work in a short time

words.example: She signed up for an intensive language course.

gloss.en: the aim or purpose of doing something

words.example: He was charged with intent to harm others.

gloss.en: allowing two-way exchange between user and device

words.example: The museum has many interactive displays for kids.

gloss.en: how a program shows or takes information

words.example: The app has a simple user interface.

gloss.en: to get involved where you are not wanted

words.example: I wish my mother would stop trying to interfere.

gloss.en: the act of getting involved where unwanted

words.example: They resent foreign interference in their affairs.

gloss.en: lasting for only a short time, temporary

words.example: The country has an interim government for now.

gloss.en: the inside part of something

words.example: They painted the interior of the house white.

gloss.en: located or coming between two things or stages

words.example: This is an intermediate stage in the process.

gloss.en: to get involved to improve a situation

words.example: She might have died if neighbors hadn't intervened.

gloss.en: action taken to improve or help a situation

words.example: There were calls for government intervention.

gloss.en: having a close, friendly relationship

words.example: They are intimate friends who share everything.

gloss.en: very interesting because unusual or mysterious
gloss.zh: 引人入胜的；耐人寻味的

words.example: These discoveries raise intriguing questions.

gloss.en: a person who examines an accident or crime

words.example: Air safety investigators checked the wreckage.

gloss.en: that cannot be seen

words.example: The wizard could make himself invisible.

gloss.en: to use a law or rule as a reason

words.example: The lawyer chose to invoke an old law.

gloss.en: the act of taking part in something

words.example: His involvement in the project was important.

gloss.en: meaning the opposite of what is said

words.example: She made an ironic comment about the weather.

gloss.en: in a way that means the opposite
gloss.zh: 讽刺地；具有讽刺意味地

words.example: He smiled ironically and said nothing.

gloss.en: a strange result very different from expected

words.example: It was one of life's little ironies.

gloss.en: not connected with or important to something

words.example: His age is completely irrelevant to the job.

gloss.en: the state of being separate or alone

words.example: The village suffers from geographical isolation.

gloss.en: connected with a court or legal judgement

words.example: The judge holds wide judicial powers.

gloss.en: the place where roads or railways meet

words.example: Turn left at the next road junction.

gloss.en: the authority to make legal decisions

words.example: The court had no jurisdiction over the case.

gloss.en: morally fair and reasonable

words.example: Everyone hoped for a just and fair decision.

gloss.en: a good reason for something

words.example: I see no justification for any further tax increase.

gloss.en: to take someone illegally and hold them

words.example: Terrorists tried to kidnap the businessman.

gloss.en: a body organ that filters waste from blood

words.example: The doctor treated his kidney infection.

gloss.en: a country ruled by a king or queen

words.example: The United Kingdom is an island nation.

gloss.en: a boy or young man

words.example: Things have changed since I was a lad.

gloss.en: a person who rents out property to others

words.example: Our landlord raised the rent again this year.

gloss.en: a building you can see and use to navigate

words.example: The tower is a famous landmark in the city.

gloss.en: the flat top of your legs when sitting

words.example: The cat sat quietly on my lap.

gloss.en: involving many people or things over a wide area

words.example: They announced a large-scale development plan.

gloss.en: a device making a powerful narrow beam of light

words.example: The doctor used a laser to cut the tissue.

gloss.en: the second of two things just mentioned

words.example: Given two choices, he chose the latter option.

gloss.en: an area of short grass in a garden or park

words.example: In summer we mow the lawn every week.

gloss.en: a claim against someone made in court

words.example: He filed a lawsuit against his record label.

gloss.en: the way parts of something are arranged

words.example: I like the clean layout of this website.

gloss.en: a hole that lets liquid or gas escape

words.example: There is a leak in the roof.

gloss.en: a long or high jump

words.example: The dancer made a graceful leap into the air.

gloss.en: money or property left to you when someone dies

words.example: They each received a legacy of five thousand dollars.

gloss.en: very famous and talked about a lot

words.example: He became a legendary figure in football.

gloss.en: a law or set of laws passed by a parliament

words.example: They passed an important piece of legislation.

gloss.en: connected with making and passing laws

words.example: The country has a legislative assembly.

gloss.en: a group with power to make laws

words.example: Voters elected a new state legislature.

gloss.en: having a fair and acceptable reason

words.example: The workers have a legitimate complaint.

gloss.en: very long, often too long, in time or size

words.example: We faced lengthy delays at the airport.

gloss.en: connected with women attracted to women

words.example: She joined the lesbian and gay community group.

gloss.en: not as great in size or importance

words.example: He chose the lesser of two evils.

gloss.en: able to cause death

words.example: She was given a lethal dose of poison.

gloss.en: legally responsible for paying for something
gloss.zh: 负有法律责任的；应负责的

words.example: You will be liable for any damage caused.

gloss.en: open and respectful of different views

words.example: He holds quite liberal views on education.

gloss.en: the act of freeing a country or person

words.example: They fought a long war of liberation.

gloss.en: freedom to live as you choose

words.example: They fought for justice and liberty.

gloss.en: to give official permission for something

words.example: The new drug is not yet licensed in the US.

gloss.en: lasting all through your life

words.example: Paul became his lifelong friend.

gloss.en: the chance of something happening

words.example: There is little likelihood of that happening.

gloss.en: an arm or a leg of a body

words.example: He was fitted with an artificial limb.

gloss.en: the people taking part in an event

words.example: The festival had an impressive line-up of speakers.

gloss.en: of, in, or like straight lines

words.example: The artist broke the rules of linear perspective.

gloss.en: to continue to exist longer than expected

words.example: The smell of her perfume lingered in the room.

gloss.en: an official or published list of things

words.example: The site offers a full listing of all airlines.

gloss.en: the ability to read and write

words.example: They launched a campaign to promote adult literacy.

gloss.en: a body organ that cleans the blood

words.example: Too much alcohol can cause liver disease.

gloss.en: a large area inside a building's entrance

words.example: We waited for her in the hotel lobby.

gloss.en: a thick piece of wood cut from a tree

words.example: He put another log on the fire.

gloss.en: a way of thinking or reasoning

words.example: I fail to see the logic behind his argument.

gloss.en: having existed for a long time
gloss.zh: 长期存在的；由来已久的

words.example: They have a long-standing relationship.

gloss.en: having been something for a long time

words.example: She introduced her long-time colleague to us.

gloss.en: to appear as a large, unclear, scary shape

words.example: A dark shape loomed up ahead of us.
